heading: Overview
text: This is the API reference for the onchain protocols PND ships for artists. Each protocol is a self-contained set of contracts with its own section below: an overview, the concepts behind it, a per-contract reference, task-oriented guides, and cross-cutting indexes of its functions, events, and error

heading: Overview
text: The PND Surface System is a modular collection protocol for artists. One artist collection is one ERC721 core, Surface: it holds ownership, one seed per token, the renderer pointer, the royalty, the supply cap, and the minter authorization, and no sale logic and no ETH. Every collection is deployed

text: Shared singletons (deployed once, used by every collection): Contract Role --- --- SurfaceFactory Clones the token and its canonical minter and wires them in one transaction DefaultRenderer The default IRenderer a freshly deployed collection points at until the artist sets something else RenderAsset

heading: Slots and modules
text: The token has one swappable slot and a minter set; the canonical minter adds a third slot: - Renderer (IRenderer) on the token: builds tokenURI; falls back to the factory defaultRenderer when unset; pinnable one-way with lockRenderer - Minter on the token: an address the artist authorizes via setMin

heading: Provenance and identity
text: Every mint stamps exactly one piece of per-token state: the token's seed (tokenSeed), a bytes32 stable across transfers, the value a generative renderer draws from and the one fact that can never be reconstructed later. The rest of a token's provenance is derived or event-recorded: in Sequential mod

heading: Live settings and the locks
text: On the token, the artist (or an admin they grant) can change the renderer (setRenderer), the EIP-2981 royalty (setRoyalty), and the supply cap (setSupplyCap), and manage minters (setMinter). On the minter, they can change the price, window, payout, sale ceiling, allowlist, and per-wallet cap. The to

heading: Id modes
text: A collection is either sequential (the core assigns ids, mintTo mints the next ones, ids are never reused) or pooled (the minter supplies every id through mintToId, a burned id can be re-minted as a new instance). Fixed at init, not changeable after. See Id modes.

heading: Pricing and the referral share
text: On the canonical minter, the required payment is the resolved price: the stored fixed price, or the price strategy's quote. Out of that price, a fixed 10% (REFERRAL SHARE BPS) goes to the referrer passed to mint. A mint with no referrer directs the full price to the payout. There is no protocol fee

The core holds ownership, one seed per token, the renderer pointer, the royalty, the supply cap, and the minter authorization, and no sale logic. What varies

When the artist sets none

The value-facing shape a minter presents to frontends is IMinter, but the token does not enforce it - Set by : setMinter(address

heading: Price strategy (`IPriceStrategy`)
text: - Type : IPriceStrategy, stored on the canonical minter as priceStrategy, not on the token - Set by : setPriceStrategy(address) on the minter, owner or admin (the collection's, borrowed by the minter), no freeze gate - Fallback : when unset, the minter charges its stored price times quantity and req

heading: Id modes
text: Every collection is one of two id modes, a structural fact fixed by which form the factory deploys (createSurface/createSurfaceCustom for sequential, createPooledSurface for pooled) and never changeable afterward. It is not a config field; it is read back with idMode(). The mode governs who assigns

heading: Sequential
text: The core assigns every id itself, counting up from 1. - The token exposes one mint entrypoint, mintTo(to, quantity), callable only by an authorized minter. It draws the next id from the core's counter; the minter can never choose one. One call mints quantity consecutive ids and emits one Minted even

heading: Pooled
text: An authorized minter supplies every id explicitly. - The token exposes one mint entrypoint, mintToId(to, tokenId), callable only by the authorized minter. tokenId is caller-supplied (the pool's own id scheme; tokenId == sourceId is the typical form, and 0 is a legal id) - burn(tokenId) is restricted

text: Access: owner or admin (onlyOwnerOrAdmin, else NotAuthorized) One-way: pins the renderer pointer, so tokenURI/contractURI are answered by the current renderer from then on. The token does not verify a renderer's internals: a locked pointer at an immutable renderer fixes presentation, while a locked

heading: lockSupply
text: Access: owner or admin (onlyOwnerOrAdmin, else NotAuthorized) One-way: freezes the supply cap. The cap bounds every mint path, so no later minter grant can exceed it. Reverts SupplyIsLocked if already locked. Emits SupplyLocked.

heading: lockMinter
text: Access: owner-only on the pooled form; owner or admin on the sequential form (else NotAuthorized) One-way: freezes the minter set, so no minter is granted or revoked afterward. On a backed pooled collection this prevents granting a later minter that could burn tokens the current minter backs. Revert

heading: addAdmin
text: Access: owner-only (onlyOwner, else OwnableUnauthorizedAccount) Grants an admin key. An admin can call every management function the owner can except managing the admin set, transferring ownership, and changing the minter set on a pooled collection. Reverts ZeroAccount for the zero address and Alrea

heading: removeAdmin
text: Access: owner, or the admin itself (else NotAuthorized) Revokes an admin. The owner may remove any admin; an admin may remove itself by passing its own address. Reverts NotAnAdmin when the account holds no grant. Emits AdminSet.

heading: notifyMetadataUpdate

The event is emitted on the token, so the renderer calls this rat

heading: initialize
text: Access: deployer one-shot (initializer, else InvalidInitialization) Sets the clone up once: name, symbol, owner, the SurfaceConfig, the factory's default renderer (used when the config names none), initial minters, the Catalog address, and an initial creator listing. Reverts OwnerRequired for a zero

heading: transferOwnership
text: Access: owner-only (onlyOwner, else OwnableUnauthorizedAccount) OpenZeppelin Ownable2Step: records a pending owner who must call acceptOwnership. Existing admin grants stop being valid once the transfer completes. Emits OwnershipTransferStarted.

heading: acceptOwnership
text: Access: pending-owner-only (else OwnableUnauthorizedAccount) Ownable2Step: the pending owner completes the transfer and becomes owner. Admin grants made by the previous owner are no longer valid. Emits OwnershipTransferred.

heading: renounceOwnership
text: Access: owner-only (onlyOwner, else OwnableUnauthorizedAccount) OpenZeppelin Ownable: sets the owner to the zero address. Every owner and admin gate then reverts (an admin grant is valid only while its granting owner is the current owner), and the config is fixed at its current values. A royalty rec

heading: approve
text: Access: owner-or-operator-only (standard ERC721 approval authority, else an ERC721 revert) Standard ERC721 single-token approval. Emits Approval.

heading: setApprovalForAll
text: Access: permissionless (the caller sets its own operator approval) Standard ERC721 operator approval over all of the caller's tokens. Emits ApprovalForAll.

heading: transferFrom
text: Access: owner-or-approved-only (standard ERC721 transfer authority, else ERC721InsufficientApproval) Standard ERC721 transfer. The mint entrypoints and burn are the non-standard surface; transfers follow EIP-721. Emits Transfer.

heading: safeTransferFrom(address, address, uint256)
text: Access: owner-or-approved-only (standard ERC721 transfer authority, else ERC721InsufficientApproval) Standard ERC721 safe transfer with no data. Checks a contract recipient implements onERC721Received (ERC721InvalidReceiver otherwise). Emits Transfer.

heading: safeTransferFrom(address, address, uint256, bytes)
text: Access: owner-or-approved-only (standard ERC721 transfer authority, else ERC721InsufficientApproval) Standard ERC721 safe transfer with a data payload forwarded to the recipient's onERC721Received. Emits Transfer.

heading: balanceOf
text: Standard ERC721: the number of tokens an address owns.

heading: catalog
text: The Catalog singleton this collection confirms creators against (zero when confirmation is disabled).

heading: config
text: Returns the live SurfaceConfig and minted, the mints-ever count (not live supply). The config fields are supplyCap (0 = open supply), royaltyBps, royaltyReceiver (0 = owner()), renderer, rendererLocked, and supplyLocked. Sale state (price, window, phase) is not here; read the collection's minter.

heading: contractURI
text: Collection-level metadata, delegated to the renderer's contractURI.

heading: getApproved
text: Standard ERC721: the single-token approved spender, or zero.

heading: idMode
text: The collection form: Sequential (0) or Pooled (1). Fixed by the contract. See id modes.

heading: isAdmin
text: True if the account may use the admin-gated setters: the owner, or an address holding a currently valid grant. The owner is included because every admin-gated function also admits the owner.

heading: isApprovedForAll
text: Standard ERC721: whether an operator is approved over all of an owner's tokens.

heading: isConfirmedCreator
text: True only if the owner has listed who and who has claimed this collection in the Catalog (isContractRegistered). Computed on read, so retracting either side revokes the confirmation. False when no Catalog is configured.

heading: isListedCreator
text: Whether the owner has listed who as a creator (the owner's assertion). See isConfirmedCreator.

heading: isMinter
text: True if the address is an authorized minter allowed to call the mint entrypoint.

heading: isMinterLocked
text: True after lockMinter.

heading: isRendererLocked
text: True after lockRenderer.

heading: isSupplyLocked
text: True after lockSupply.

heading: name
text: Standard ERC721 collection name, set at init.

heading: owner
text: Standard OpenZeppelin Ownable owner: the address that controls the config, the minter set, and the admin set.

heading: ownerOf
text: Standard ERC721: the owner of a token. Reverts ERC721NonexistentToken for an id that is not currently minted.

heading: pendingOwner
text: Standard Ownable2Step pending owner: the address offered ownership that must call acceptOwnership, or zero when no transfer is pending.

heading: renderer
text: The renderer address tokenURI and contractURI delegate to. Set at init (the config's value, or the factory default when the config names none) and settable via setRenderer until lockRenderer.

heading: royaltyInfo
text: EIP-2981 royalty for a sale price: the receiver (the configured receiver, or owner() when unset) and the amount from royaltyBps. Advisory.

heading: supportsInterface
text: Standard ERC165 check. True for ERC721, ERC165, EIP-2981 (0x2a55205a), and ERC-4906 (0x49064906).

heading: symbol
text: Standard ERC721 collection symbol, set at init.

heading: tokenSeed
text: The seed for a token, written in the mint transaction: the only per-token storage. Reverts NeverMinted for an id that was never minted (a nonzero seed is the existence sentinel). Readable for a burned id until a pooled re-mint overwrites it with the new instance's seed.

heading: tokenURI
text: Standard ERC721 metadata entry point. Reverts ERC721NonexistentToken for a token that does not exist and delegates to the renderer's tokenURI.

heading: totalSupply
text: Live supply: mints ever minus burns. On the sequential form a burn lowers this; on the pooled form a re-mint of a burned id raises it.

heading: version
text: The implementation version, a compile-time constant (1 for this generation). A new generation ships as a new implementation and factory, so a collection reports the version it was cloned from.

heading: AdminSet
text: Emitted when an admin key is granted (allowed = true) or revoked. Indexed by account.

heading: Approval
text: Standard ERC721 single-token approval event. Indexed by owner, approved, and tokenId.

heading: ApprovalForAll
text: Standard ERC721 operator approval event. Indexed by owner and operator, with the approved flag.

heading: BatchMetadataUpdate
text: ERC-4906 range refresh, emitted by setRenderer and setSupplyCap (all tokens) and by notifyMetadataUpdate (a renderer- or admin-chosen range).

heading: Burned
text: Emitted when a token is burned. Indexed by tokenId. A pooled id may be re-minted later, covered by a new Minted.

heading: ContractURIUpdated
text: ERC-7572 contract-level refresh, emitted by setRenderer alongside the token-range refresh.

heading: CreatorListed
text: Emitted when the owner lists or unlists a creator, including each creator seeded at init. Indexed by creator, with the listed flag. Confirmed status is a live isConfirmedCreator read.

heading: Initialized
text: OpenZeppelin Initializable event, emitted once when the clone is initialized.

heading: MetadataUpdate
text: ERC-4906 single-token refresh, declared for interface completeness; range refreshes use BatchMetadataUpdate.

heading: Minted
text: Emitted once per mint call. Indexed by minter (the authorized minter that issued the tokens) and to (the recipient). On the sequential form a call covers ids [firstTokenId, firstTokenId + quantity - 1]; on the pooled form firstTokenId is the minted id and quantity is 1. firstMintIndex is the global

heading: MinterLocked
text: Emitted once when lockMinter runs.

heading: MinterSet
text: Emitted when a minter is granted or revoked, and once per initial minter at init. Indexed by minter, with the allowed flag. The factory's SurfaceCreated names the canonically wired minter directly.

heading: OwnershipTransferred
text: Standard Ownable event, emitted at init when the first owner is set, when acceptOwnership completes a transfer, and when renounceOwnership sets the owner to zero. Indexed by previousOwner and newOwner.

heading: OwnershipTransferStarted
text: Standard Ownable2Step event, emitted by transferOwnership when a pending owner is recorded. Indexed by previousOwner and newOwner.

heading: RendererLocked
text: Emitted once when lockRenderer runs, or for a lock set at init.

heading: RendererSet
text: Emitted when the renderer pointer changes. Indexed by renderer.

heading: RoyaltySet
text: Emitted when the royalty changes with setRoyalty. Indexed by royaltyReceiver.

heading: StrayETHRescued
text: Emitted when rescueStrayETH sweeps force-fed ETH. Indexed by to, with the amount in wei.

heading: SupplyCapSet
text: Emitted when the supply cap changes with setSupplyCap.

heading: SupplyLocked
text: Emitted once when lockSupply runs, or for a lock set at init.

heading: SurfaceConfigured
text: Emitted once at init with the id mode and supply cap. The id mode is fixed; the cap has its own update event.

heading: Transfer
text: Standard ERC721 transfer event, emitted on mint (from the zero address), transfer, and burn (to the zero address). Indexed by from, to, and tokenId.

heading: The context object

heading: Determinism rules for pure works

heading: Overview
text: The Sovereign Auction House is an onchain English auction house for ERC721 tokens, denominated in ETH. Every seller (an artist or a collector) runs their own house: it is deployed per owner as an immutable EIP-1167 clone, with isolated storage and no shared custody. It auctions any ERC721, including

heading: The two contracts
text: - SovereignAuctionHouse: the per-owner clone that holds the escrowed NFTs, runs the auctions, and pays out. Its owner is fixed at init and cannot be changed: transferOwnership and renounceOwnership revert. Each house has no single fixed address, so examples use an <AUCTION HOUSE ADDRESS placeholder

heading: Status
text: The auction house is pre-deploy. The factory is a shared singleton referenced as <AUCTION HOUSE FACTORY ADDRESS in examples, which resolves to its mainnet address once deployed. Each house is a per-owner clone with no fixed address, so its examples use <AUCTION HOUSE ADDRESS . Read examples use cast

heading: Bid
text: A bidder calls createBid and sends ETH as the bid (msg.value). text: If a bid lands inside the last TIME BUFFER (15 minutes) before the end time, the house pushes the end time out to block.timestamp + TIME BUFFER. This guarantees every bidder at least 15 minutes to respond to a late bid, so an auction cannot be won by sniping the final block. The extending bid emits

heading: End (settle)
text: Once block.timestamp reaches the end time and the auction has at least one bid, anyone can call endAuction. The house: 1. transfers the NFT to the winning bidder 2. takes the protocol fee (protocolFeeBps of the winning bid) for feeRecipient 3. sends the remaining proceeds to the seller An auction th

heading: Cancel
text: Before the first bid, the seller can cancelAuction to pull the escrowed NFT back out. Once a bid has landed the auction is a live commitment to its bidders and can no longer be cancelled (AuctionAlreadyStarted). The house owner can cancel many pre-bid auctions at once with bulkCancelAuctions.

Reverts to required for a zero destination, and AuctionAlreadyExistsForToken if the to

Reverts owner required for a zero owner, fee above cap for a fee over 500 bps (5%), and fee recipient required when fee 0 when a non-zero fee has no re

heading: receive
text: Access: permissionless (payable, but always reverts) Rejects any direct ETH transfer with "Direct ETH not accepted". Bids must go through createBid, which ties the payment to a specific auction; a bare transfer has no auction to credit. Forced ETH (selfdestruct, coinbase) is outside the contract's c

heading: auctions
text: The full stored state of an auction by id: tokenId, tokenContract, firstBidTime (0 before any bid), current high amount, reservePrice, seller tokenOwner, endTime (0 until the first bid), current high bidder, and duration in seconds. A zero tokenOwner means no auction exists at that id.

heading: feeRecipient
text: The address that receives the protocol fee at settlement, fixed at init. It is the zero address only when protocolFeeBps is zero.

heading: getAuctionFor
text: Resolves a (tokenContract, tokenId) to its live auction: returns an exists flag and the auctionId. The tuple shape disambiguates "no auction" from "auction id 0", which are otherwise indistinguishable.

heading: getMinBidAmount
text: The minimum next bid in wei for an auction, paired with an exists flag so a caller can tell "no auction" apart from "minimum is zero". Mirrors exactly what createBid enforces: the reserve for the first bid, else the current high bid plus the MIN BID INCREMENT BPS increment with the 1-wei floor.

heading: hasAuctionFor
text: A cheap boolean check for whether a token is currently in an auction on this house, when the auctionId itself is not needed.

heading: MIN_BID_INCREMENT_BPS
text: The minimum increment a later bid must add over the current high bid, as a compile-time constant: 500 bps (5%). Not owner-set. A 1-wei floor applies when the bps math on a tiny bid rounds to zero.

heading: nextAuctionId
text: The auction id that the next createAuction call will assign. Useful for clients that want to pre-compute the id before broadcasting the transaction.

heading: owner
text: The house owner, fixed at init: the address that creates and cancels auctions and can recover stuck NFTs. Ownership is locked, so this value never changes.

heading: pendingRefunds
text: The pull-payment balance in wei currently owed to an address, from an outbid refund or a settlement payout whose push failed. Claimable with withdrawRefund.

heading: protocolFeeBps
text: The protocol fee in basis points taken from each winning bid at settlement, fixed at init and capped at 500 (5%).

heading: renounceOwnership
text: Disabled: this function is pure and always reverts OwnershipLocked. Renouncing would set the owner to zero and disable every owner-only function, so it is permanently disabled alongside transferOwnership.

heading: TIME_BUFFER
text: The anti-snipe window as a compile-time constant: 15 minutes. A bid inside the last TIME BUFFER before the end time pushes the end time out to block.timestamp + TIME BUFFER.

heading: transferOwnership
text: Disabled: this function is pure and always reverts OwnershipLocked. Ownership is fixed at init so the house can never be reassigned away from its original owner, which keeps the factory's owner-to-house map stable and keeps a house bound to the NFTs it escrows.

heading: AuctionBid
text: Emitted on every bid. Indexed by auctionId and bidder, and carries the bid amount, a firstBid flag for the bid that starts the timer, and an extended flag for a bid inside TIME BUFFER that pushed the end time out.

heading: AuctionCanceled
text: Emitted when a pending auction is cancelled and its NFT returned to the seller. Indexed by auctionId.

heading: AuctionCreated
text: Emitted when an auction is registered and its NFT escrowed. Indexed by auctionId, tokenId, and tokenContract, and carries the duration, reservePrice, and seller tokenOwner. This is the event an indexer reads to record a new listing.

heading: AuctionEnded
text: Emitted when an auction settles. Indexed by auctionId, and carries the seller tokenOwner, the winner, the sellerProceeds, and the protocolFee, so an indexer can recover the full payout breakdown without re-running the fee math.

heading: AuctionEndTimeUpdated
text: Emitted when a late bid pushed the auction end time out. Indexed by auctionId, with the newEndTime. Fires alongside the AuctionBid whose extended flag is set.

heading: AuctionReservePriceUpdated
text: Emitted when the seller updates the reserve before the first bid lands. Indexed by auctionId, with the new reservePrice.

heading: Initialized
text: Standard OpenZeppelin Initializable event, emitted once when the clone is initialized.

heading: OwnershipTransferred
text: Standard OpenZeppelin Ownable event, emitted once at init when the house's owner is set from the zero address. Ownership is locked afterward, so it never fires again. Indexed by previousOwner and newOwner.

heading: RefundCredited
text: Emitted when an ETH push fails and the amount is credited to a withdrawable pendingRefunds balance instead. Indexed by to, with the credited amount. Applies to outbid refunds, seller proceeds, and the protocol fee.

heading: RefundWithdrawn
text: Emitted when a recipient claims their credited refund with withdrawRefund. Indexed by to, with the amount paid out.

heading: StuckERC721Recovered
text: Emitted when the house owner recovers a misdirected ERC721 that landed on the house outside the auction flow. Indexed by tokenContract and tokenId, with the to destination.

heading: Errors
text: AuctionAlreadyExistsForToken() A second auction was attempted for a (tokenContract, tokenId) that already has a live one on this house, or recoverStuckERC721 targeted a token that is currently in an auction. Cancel or settle the first to free the slot. AuctionAlreadyStarted() An action that requires

heading: Deploy an auction house
text: An auction house is deployed per owner. You get your own house by calling createAuctionHouse on the shared SovereignAuctionHouseFactory. The new house is owned by you (the caller), initialized with the factory's default fee terms, and deployed at an address predictable from your address alone.

heading: One house per owner
text: Each address can have exactly one house from this factory. A second createAuctionHouse call from the same address reverts House already exists. Check whether you already have one with houseOf: A zero address means you have not deployed one yet.

heading: Predict the address first
text: The house address is deterministic from the owner address, so you can show or use it before the deploy transaction lands: Do not send ETH or NFTs to the predicted address before the house exists: funds sent there pre-deploy are not recoverable. Deploy the house first, then interact with it.

heading: The init defaults
text: Every house this factory deploys is initialized with the factory's own fee terms, which are fixed at the factory's construction and shared across all its houses: - defaultFeeRecipient: the address that receives the protocol fee at settlement - defaultProtocolFeeBps: the protocol fee in basis points,

heading: Deploy with cast
text: The call returns your house address and emits AuctionHouseCreated with your address and the house both indexed.

heading: Overview
text: Catalog is a general onchain registry where an artist address publishes pointers to its work. It is deployed on Ethereum mainnet and is not specific to the Surface System: any address may register any contract or token. A pointer is one of three types: - a contract address - a token , a (contract, t

heading: What a catalog entry means
text: An entry records only that a given address added a given pointer. It does not prove authorship, provenance, ownership, or creator status, and does not check that the referenced contract or token exists or behaves as an NFT. heading: Catalog
text: 0x467a9c39e03C595EC3075D856f19C7386b6b915d · view on evm.now · Ethereum mainnet An immutable public registry where an artist address publishes onchain pointers to its work. A pointer is one of three types: a contract address, a single token on a contract, or a contiguous inclusive range of token ids

heading: Pointer types
text: Three independent pointer types, each stored in its own per-artist list and keyed separately: - contract : an address (addContract). Any address is accepted, including an EOA; the only check is nonzero - token : a (contract, tokenId) pair (addToken) - token range : a (contract, startTokenId, endToke

heading: Direct and operator writes
text: Every mutation has a direct form that writes the caller's own list (addContract) and a For form that writes a named artist's list (addContractFor(artist, ...)). The For form reverts NotAuthorized unless the caller is the artist or an operator the artist approved with setOperator. An operator cannot

heading: Unordered storage
text: Removal is swap-and-pop: removing an entry that is not last moves the last entry into the freed slot. Order is therefore not stable across writes. A reader needing a fixed order sorts client-side. Existence and removal are O(1) through an internal index map, so an unordered list is not a scan.

heading: Batching with multicall
text: The contract inherits OpenZeppelin Multicall. multicall(bytes[] data) runs several encoded calls in one transaction, each by delegatecall to this contract so msg.sender is preserved and the For authorization check applies inside a batch exactly as outside it. The batch is atomic: the first inner rev

heading: Per-chain deterministic deployment
text: Each Catalog instance is scoped to its deployment chain; pointers reference contracts on that same chain, and there is no chainId field. Deploying at the same address on multiple chains requires the CREATE2 deterministic-deployment proxy plus a matching salt, init code, compiler version, optimizer s

heading: addContract
text: Access: permissionless (writes only the caller's own list) Adds a contract pointer to the caller's catalog. Reverts InvalidContractAddress for the zero address and ContractAlreadyRegistered if the caller already registered it. Emits ContractAdded.

heading: addContractFor
text: Access: artist or operator (the artist argument, or an address it approved via setOperator, else NotAuthorized) Adds a contract pointer to artist's catalog. Reverts InvalidArtist for a zero artist, InvalidContractAddress for a zero contract, and ContractAlreadyRegistered on a duplicate. Emits Contra

heading: removeContract
text: Access: permissionless (writes only the caller's own list) Removes a contract pointer from the caller's catalog. Reverts ContractNotRegistered if the caller has no such pointer. Emits ContractRemoved.

heading: removeContractFor
text: Access: artist or operator (the artist argument, or an approved operator, else NotAuthorized) Removes a contract pointer from artist's catalog. Reverts InvalidArtist for a zero artist and ContractNotRegistered if the pointer does not exist. Emits ContractRemoved.

heading: addToken
text: Access: permissionless (writes only the caller's own list) Adds a single-token pointer (contract, tokenId) to the caller's catalog. Reverts InvalidContractAddress for a zero contract and TokenAlreadyRegistered on a duplicate. Emits TokenAdded.

heading: addTokenFor
text: Access: artist or operator (the artist argument, or an approved operator, else NotAuthorized) Adds a single-token pointer to artist's catalog. Reverts InvalidArtist, InvalidContractAddress, or TokenAlreadyRegistered as above. Emits TokenAdded.

heading: removeToken
text: Access: permissionless (writes only the caller's own list) Removes a single-token pointer from the caller's catalog. Reverts TokenNotRegistered if absent. Emits TokenRemoved.

heading: removeTokenFor
text: Access: artist or operator (the artist argument, or an approved operator, else NotAuthorized) Removes a single-token pointer from artist's catalog. Reverts InvalidArtist or TokenNotRegistered. Emits TokenRemoved.

heading: addTokenRange
text: Access: permissionless (writes only the caller's own list) Adds a token-range pointer (contract, startTokenId, endTokenId) to the caller's catalog. Reverts InvalidContractAddress for a zero contract, InvalidTokenRange when startTokenId endTokenId, and TokenRangeAlreadyRegistered on a duplicate tuple

heading: addTokenRangeFor
text: Access: artist or operator (the artist argument, or an approved operator, else NotAuthorized) Adds a token-range pointer to artist's catalog. Reverts InvalidArtist, InvalidContractAddress, InvalidTokenRange, or TokenRangeAlreadyRegistered as above. Emits TokenRangeAdded.

heading: removeTokenRange
text: Access: permissionless (writes only the caller's own list) Removes a token-range pointer from the caller's catalog. Reverts InvalidTokenRange for an inverted range (rejected on the same terms as add) and TokenRangeNotRegistered if the exact tuple is absent. Emits TokenRangeRemoved.

heading: removeTokenRangeFor
text: Access: artist or operator (the artist argument, or an approved operator, else NotAuthorized) Removes a token-range pointer from artist's catalog. Reverts InvalidArtist, InvalidTokenRange, or TokenRangeNotRegistered. Emits TokenRangeRemoved.

heading: setOperator
text: Access: permissionless (sets only the caller's own operator slot) Approves or revokes operator for the caller, controlling isOperator[msg.sender][operator]. An approved operator may call the For functions on the caller's behalf. There is no setOperatorFor, so an operator cannot sub-delegate. Reverts

heading: multicall
text: Access: permissionless (each inner call enforces its own authorization) Runs several encoded calls in one transaction, each by delegatecall to this contract, so msg.sender is preserved and the For authorization applies to inner calls. Atomic: the first inner revert reverts the whole batch. Returns e

heading: getCatalogCountsOf
text: The three list lengths for artist (contracts, tokens, token ranges) in one call. An address with no entries returns (0, 0, 0). Cheaper than getCatalogOf when only sizes are needed.

heading: getCatalogOf
text: Every pointer in artist's catalog in one call: the contract list, the single-token list, and the token-range list. A zero or empty artist returns three empty arrays. For a large catalog, prefer the per-type Slice getters to stay within RPC return-size limits.

heading: getContractAt
text: The contract pointer at index in artist's list. Reverts on an out-of-bounds index. The list order is not stable across writes.

heading: getContractCount
text: The number of contract pointers in artist's catalog.

heading: getContractKey
text: The internal storage key for a contract pointer, keccak256(abi.encode(contractAddress)). Exposed for callers that reproduce the keying offchain.

heading: getContracts
text: Every contract pointer in artist's catalog. Order is not stable; for a large list prefer getContractsSlice.

heading: getContractsSlice
text: Up to count contract pointers from artist's list starting at start. A start past the end returns an empty array; a start + count past the end returns the remaining elements. For paginated reads without copying the full list.

heading: getTokenAt
text: The single-token pointer at index in artist's list, returned as (contractAddress, tokenId). Reverts on an out-of-bounds index.

heading: getTokenCount
text: The number of single-token pointers in artist's catalog.

heading: getTokenKey
text: The internal storage key for a single-token pointer, keccak256(abi.encode(contractAddress, tokenId)).

heading: getTokenRangeAt
text: The token-range pointer at index in artist's list, returned as (contractAddress, startTokenId, endTokenId). Reverts on an out-of-bounds index.

heading: getTokenRangeCount
text: The number of token-range pointers in artist's catalog.

heading: getTokenRangeKey
text: The internal storage key for a token-range pointer, keccak256(abi.encode(contractAddress, startTokenId, endTokenId)).

heading: getTokenRanges
text: Every token-range pointer in artist's catalog, as (contract, startTokenId, endTokenId) structs. Order is not stable; for a large list prefer getTokenRangesSlice.

heading: getTokenRangesSlice
text: Up to count token-range pointers from artist's list starting at start, with the same out-of-range handling as getContractsSlice.

heading: getTokens
text: Every single-token pointer in artist's catalog, as (contract, tokenId) structs. Order is not stable; for a large list prefer getTokensSlice.

heading: getTokensSlice
text: Up to count single-token pointers from artist's list starting at start, with the same out-of-range handling as getContractsSlice.

heading: isContractRegistered
text: Whether artist has a contract pointer for contractAddress.

heading: isOperator
text: Whether artist has approved operator to write its lists through the For functions.

heading: isTokenRangeRegistered
text: Whether artist has a token-range pointer for the exact (contractAddress, startTokenId, endTokenId) tuple. Does not report ranges that merely cover those bounds; coverage is a reader-side computation.

heading: isTokenRegistered
text: Whether artist has a single-token pointer for (contractAddress, tokenId).

heading: ContractAdded
text: Emitted when a contract pointer is added. Indexed by artist (whose list changed), actor (msg.sender), and contractAddress. actor != artist on an operator write.

heading: ContractRemoved
text: Emitted when a contract pointer is removed. Indexed by artist, actor, and contractAddress.

heading: OperatorSet
text: Emitted on every setOperator call, including a no-op that sets the current value. Indexed by artist and operator, with the new approved flag.

heading: TokenAdded
text: Emitted when a single-token pointer is added. Indexed by artist, actor, and contractAddress; tokenId is in the data segment (three topic slots are already used).

heading: TokenRangeAdded
text: Emitted when a token-range pointer is added. Indexed by artist, actor, and contractAddress, with startTokenId and endTokenId in the data.

heading: TokenRangeRemoved
text: Emitted when a token-range pointer is removed. Indexed by artist, actor, and contractAddress, with startTokenId and endTokenId in the data.

heading: TokenRemoved
text: Emitted when a single-token pointer is removed. Indexed by artist, actor, and contractAddress, with tokenId in the data.
